# @andes/tag

Tags are compact components that represent complex information in a summarized way. Allowing users to make selections, enter information, or filter content.

The UI specs are defined in [Andes UI](http://andes.adminml.com) > [Tag](https://andes.mercadolibre.com/924dc6ec5/p/6332d2-tags/b/26f535).

## How to use

### Installation

> Follow the [general installation guide](../../docs/installation.md).

```bash
npm install --save @andes/tag
```

### Styles

> Follow the [general styles usage guide](../../docs/styles-usage.md) for configuration and how-tos.

Import the component in your bundle:

```scss
@import '@andes/tag/index';
@import '@andes/thumbnail/index';
```

Or you can import only the styles that you need in your project like this:

```scss
// Base
@import '@andes/tag/base';

// Colors
// positive
@import '@andes/tag/lib/styles/color/positive';
// caution
@import '@andes/tag/lib/styles/color/caution';
// negative
@import '@andes/tag/lib/styles/color/negative';

// Tag collapsed
@import '@andes/tag/lib/styles/tagCollapsed';

// Tag choice
@import '@andes/tag/lib/styles/tagChoice';
```

### Scripts

> Follow the [general scripts usage guide](../../docs/scripts-usage.md) for configuration and how-tos.

Import the component in your page:

```jsx
import { Tag, TagChoice, TagCollapsed } from '@andes/tag';
```

### Assets

The asset precedence is the following one: image, icon, text, none.

That means if you do not provide the `imageUrl` property or there was an error loading the image, the component will try to show an icon, if there's no `icon` property it will try to show a text, and if there's no `text` property it will show no avatar.

If the image aspect ratio is not 1:1 and the image original size is greater than the container it will be cut to fit the container.

## Examples

For full demo and configuration options, check out the [story for Tag](https://andes.adminml.com/web/?path=/story/tag--andes-default-tag).

### Tag

```jsx
<Tag
  label="Lorem ipsum"
  asset={{
    imageUrl: 'https://mercadolibre.com/some-image.png',
    icon: <MyIcon />,
    text: 'LI',
  }}
/>
```

### TagCollapsed

In cases where screen space is limited, it is possible to group multiple tags into a single one that represents more than one value.

Import the component in your page:

```jsx
import { Tag, TagCollapsed } from '@andes/tag';
```

```jsx
<TagCollapsed limit={2} srLabel={i18n.gettext('Tag seleccionados')}>
  <Tag label="tag 1" />
  <Tag label="tag 2" />
  <Tag label="tag 3" />
</TagCollapsed>
```

### TagChoice

Uncontrolled mode

```jsx
<TagChoice label="Lorem ipsum" defaultSelected />
```

Controlled mode

```jsx
const [selected, setSelected] = useState(false);

const handleClick = () => setSelected(!selected)

<TagChoice label="Lorem ipsum" onClick={handleClick} selected={selected} />
```

## Props

### Tag

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `asset` | Custom asset configuration | `Object<AssetConfig>` | `undefined` |  |
| `children` | Shows a custom element inside the tag | `node` | `undefined` |  |
| `className` | Custom class | `string` | `''` |  |
| `clickable` | Sets a pointer cursor over the tag | `bool` | `false` |  |
| `closeButtonSrLabel` | <sup>1</sup>Close button screen reader label | `string` | `undefined` |
| `color` | <sup>2</sup>Applies a color over the tag to show different states | `['negative', 'positive', 'caution']`| `undefined` |  |
| `component` | A string to use a specific HTML element as the root node | `elementType` | `div` |  |
| `disabled` | Applies disabled styles to tag, and disables any action | `bool` | `false` |  |
| `id` | Unique identifier | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `label` | Label to show in the tag | `string` |  | ✅ |
| `onClick` | <sup>3</sup>Callback that triggers when the tag is clicked | `function(event)` | `undefined` |  |
| `onClose` | <sup>4</sup>Callback that triggers when the close button is clicked | `function(event)` | `undefined` |  |
| `size` | Tag size | `'small', 'large'`| `'large'` |  |
| `srLabel` | Label for screen readers | `string` | `undefined` |  |

#### AssetConfig

```jsx
  {
    icon: element, // Shows an icon on asset
    imageUrl: string, // Shows an image on asset
    text: string, // Shows a text on asset
  }
```

> 1 - Andes provides an internal i18n solution. If you need a closeButton label different than default key (`cerrar {label}` | `fechar {label}`), this prop is available to overwrite it. Follow the example below:

```jsx
<Tag
  label={i18n.gettext('Productos mejor puntuados')}
  closeButtonLabel={i18n.gettext('Eliminar filtro: Productos mejor puntuados')}
  onClose={() => alert('Tag closed')}
/>
```

> 2 - Colors `red`, `orange` and `green` options are deprecated. Please, use `negative`, `caution` and `positive` instead.

> 3 - TYPESCRIPT: When `onClick` prop is passed, by default Tag returns a button element, so `component` prop needs to be used for the reference to inherit the correct type. By default `component="button"` should be used.

> 4 - `onClick` and `onClose` props are mutually exclusive. So when using `onClick`, if `onClose` was passed, then this callback will have priority.

### TagChoice

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `asset` | Custom asset configuration | `Object<AssetConfig>` | `undefined` |  |
| `className` | Custom class | `string` | `''` |  |
| `defaultSelected` | Whether the tag should be selected or not by default (uncontrolled mode) | `bool`| `undefined` |  |
| `id` | Unique identifier | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `label` | Label to show in the tag | `string` |  | ✅ |
| `onClick` | Callback that triggers when the tag is clicked. selected indicates the state of the tag. | `function(event, selected)` | `undefined` |  |
| `selected` | Whether the tag should be selected or not (controlled mode) | `bool`| `undefined` |  |
| `size` | Tag size | `'small', 'large'`| `'large'` |  |
| `srLabel` | Label for screen readers | `string` | `undefined` |  |

#### AssetConfig

```jsx
  {
    icon: element, // Shows an icon on asset
    imageUrl: string, // Shows an image on asset
    text: string, // Shows a text on asset
    color: Color, // custom color definition. See type Color in index.d.ts for object details.
  }
```

### Tag Collapsed

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `children` | Custom content, should be a collection of `<Tag>` | `node` | `` | |
| `disabled` | Whether the expand button should be disabled | `boolean` | `false` |  |
| `id` | Unique identifier | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `limit` | Limit of tags to show | `number` | `2` |  |
| `srLabel` | Label to describe the tag content list for screen readers | `string` | `undefined` |  |

## Theming

This component supports theming. It allows you to pass a `customTheme` object to it adjusting colors, typography, spacing and styles to align with a brand's visual identity, user preferences or project requirements.

<br />

The `customTheme` object can make use of values ​​referenced in the component or CSS properties for quick customization or make use of style overrides according to `data-attributes` specific to the component for more complex cases. We do not recommend the latter since it will be deprecated in the future.

### Using Referenced Component Values

The Tag component allows you to modify the following attributes depending on its reference value.

| Sub Attribute | Description             |
| ------------- | ----------------------- |
| `fill`        | Tag fill color.         |
| `text`        | Tag text color.         |
| `close`       | Tag close icon color.   |
| `icon`        | Tag custom icon color.  |
| `border`      | Tag border color.       |

Same with the TagChoice component, under the key `choice` with the following attributes:

| Sub Attribute | Description                   |
| ------------- | ----------------------------- |
| `fill`        | TagChoice fill color.         |
| `text`        | TagChoice text color.         |
| `check`       | TagChoice check icon color.   |
| `icon`        | TagChoice custom icon color.  |
| `border`      | TagChoice border color.       |

```jsx
const customTheme = {
  components: {
    tag: {
      color: {
        fill: {
          [status]: fillColorValue,
        },
        text: {
          [status]: {
            [type]: textColorValue,
          }          
        },
        close: {
          [status]: {
            [type]: closeColorValue,
          }
        },
        icon: {
          [status]: {
            [type]: iconColorValue
          }
        },
        border: {
          [status]: {
            [type]: borderColorValue
          }
        },
      },
      choice: {
        color: {
          [choiceState]: {
            fill: {
              [status]: fillColorChoiceValue,
            },
            text: {
              [status]: {
                [type]: textColorChoiceValue,
              }          
            },
            check: {
              [status]: {
                [type]: checkColorChoiceValue
              }
            },
            icon: {
              [status]: {
                [type]: iconColorChoiceValue
              }
            },
            border: {
              [status]: {
                [type]: borderColorChoiceValue
              }
            }, 
          },
        },
      },
    },
  },
};
```

The possible case values ​​are:

```jsx

status: default | hover | active | disabled;

```

```jsx

type: positive | caution | negative;

```

```jsx

choiceState: selected | unselected;

```

#### Example

<img src="resources/tag/Tag-and-TagChoice-Example-Reference-Theming.png" alt="Reference Theming Tag and TagChoice Example" width="300" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { Tag, TagChoice } from '@andes/tag';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import './styles.scss';
import Camera16 from '@andes/icons/Camera16';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    tag: {
      color: {
        fill: {
          default: 'yellow',
        },
        text: {
          default: 'violet',
          disabled: 'cyan',
        },
        border: {
          default: 'violet',
          disabled: 'cyan',
        }
      },
      choice: {
        color: {
          selected: {
            border: {
              default: 'magenta',
            },
            text: {
              default: 'magenta',
            },
            check: {
              default: 'magenta',
            },
            icon: {
              default: 'magenta',
            }
          },
          unselected: {
            border: {
              default: 'violet',
            },
            text: {
              default: 'violet',
            },
            icon: {
              default: 'violet',
            }
          },
        },
      },
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <Tag label="This is a styled tag"/>
    <br />
    <br />
    <Tag disabled label="...and a disabled one"/>
    <br />
    <br />
    <TagChoice label="TagChoice unselected" />
    <br />
    <br />
    <TagChoice label="TagChoice selected" defaultSelected />
    <br />
    <br />
    <TagChoice label="TagChoice with icon unselected" asset={{ icon: <Camera16 /> }} />
    <br />
    <br />
    <TagChoice label="TagChoice with icon selected" asset={{ icon: <Camera16 /> }} defaultSelected />
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/tag/index';
@import '@andes/thumbnail/index';
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.

### Using CSS Properties

The component allows passing CSS properties following this structure:

```jsx
const customTheme = {
  components: {
    tag: {
      [cssProperty]: propertyValue,
    },
  },
};
```

> Please note that styles applied in this way will impact all Andes tag regardless of their variant. Additionally, they have higher precedence than styles applied using referenced component values.

#### Example

<img src="resources/tag/Tag-and-TagChoice-Example-CSS-Properties-Theming.png" alt="CSS Properties Theming Tag and TagChoice Example" width="300" />


```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { Tag, TagChoice } from '@andes/tag';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import './styles.scss';
import Camera16 from '@andes/icons/Camera16';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    tag: {
      fontStyle: 'italic',
      fontWeight: 'bold',
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <Tag label="This is a styled tag"/>
    <br />
    <br />
    <Tag disabled label="...and a disabled one"/>
    <br />
    <br />
    <TagChoice label="TagChoice unselected" />
    <br />
    <br />
    <TagChoice label="TagChoice selected" defaultSelected />
    <br />
    <br />
    <TagChoice label="TagChoice with icon unselected" asset={{ icon: <Camera16 /> }} />
    <br />
    <br />
    <TagChoice label="TagChoice with icon selected" asset={{ icon: <Camera16 /> }} defaultSelected />
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/tag/index';
@import '@andes/thumbnail/index';
```

### Using `styleOverrides`

This component has a series of `data-attributes` that serve as a hook to style complex cases:

| data-attribute                      | Description                                                 |
| ----------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| `data-andes-tag`                    | Component tag.                                              |
| `data-andes-tag-type`               | Tag type. Can be `'simple'` or `'choice'`                   |
| `data-andes-tag-size`               | Tag size. Can be `'small'` or `'large'`.                    |
| `data-andes-tag-close`              | Close button.                                               |
| `data-andes-tag-color`              | Tag color. Can be `'positive'`, `'caution'`, `'negative'`.  |
| `data-andes-tag-collapsed-ellipse`  | Ellipse component tag for `TagCollapsed`.                   |
| `data-andes-tag-check-icon`         | Check icon for `TagChoice`.                                 |
| `data-andes-state`                  | Tag state. Can be `'disabled'` or `'selected'`.             |

Taking advantage of the them, you can use an entry called `styleOverrides` where you can add styles according to any `data-attribute`.

#### Example

<img src="resources/tag/Tag-and-TagChoice-Example-Style-Overrides-Theming.png" alt="Style Overrides Theming Tag and TagChoice Example" width="300" />


```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { Tag, TagChoice } from '@andes/tag';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import './styles.scss';
import Camera16 from '@andes/icons/Camera16';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    tag: {
      styleOverrides: {
        '&[data-andes-tag="true"]': {
          boxShadow: '3px 3px 4px #888',
        },
        '&[data-andes-state="disabled"]': {
          boxShadow: '3px 3px 12px #ddd',
        },
      },
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <Tag label="This is a styled tag"/>
    <br />
    <br />
    <Tag disabled label="...and a disabled one"/>
    <br />
    <br />
    <TagChoice label="TagChoice unselected" />
    <br />
    <br />
    <TagChoice label="TagChoice selected" defaultSelected />
    <br />
    <br />
    <TagChoice label="TagChoice with icon unselected" asset={{ icon: <Camera16 /> }} />
    <br />
    <br />
    <TagChoice label="TagChoice with icon selected" asset={{ icon: <Camera16 /> }} defaultSelected />
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/tag/index';
@import '@andes/thumbnail/index';
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.
